decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Since geometry changes are hitting legality limits and recent improvements have stalled, the strategy shifts to material tuning. Increasing the heat spreader conductivity is the highest-priority lever to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the hotspot.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution away from the chip center, directly addressing the high severity temperature violation.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions rather than the spreader itself.
Ensure the new conductivity value remains within physical plausibility for advanced thermal materials (e.g., diamond composites).
